gui.tutorials.htmlHello.category: Left पर blocks panel में HTML5 category पर click करो। तुम्हें web page बनाने के लिए ज़रूरी सारे blocks दिखेंगे।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.clientServer: जब तुम youtube.com अपने browser में type करते हो, तो browser एक quick message भेजता है दूर के एक computer को जो हमेशा on रहता है — उस computer को SERVER कहते हैं. Server के पास YouTube की सारी HTML files saved होती हैं. वो एक file तुम्हारे browser को वापस भेज देता है. तुम्हारा browser उस HTML को read करके page तुम्हारी screen पर draw कर देता है. हर site इसी तरह काम करती है — Google, तुम्हारे school की website, तुम्हारी favourite game site. CodeSkool थोड़ा special है — तुम्हारा HTML यहीं इस editor में रहता है जब तुम बना रहे हो. एक बार page ready हो जाए तो तुम उसे save करके किसी real server पर भी डाल सकते हो — और फिर दुनिया में कोई भी उसे visit कर सकता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.dragHtml: HTML5 flyout के top पर html block ढूँढो और workspace में drag करो। हर web page इसी block से शुरू होता है।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.htmlTab: Right panel पर HTML tab पर click करो। तुम्हें वो real HTML code दिख सकता है जो तुम्हारे blocks ने generate किया!
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.htmlTabAnnotated: देखो तुम्हारे blocks ने जो असली HTML बनाया। हर C-shape block एक tag pair बन गया — एक open tag angle brackets के साथ, बीच में तुम्हारा content, और एक matching close tag slash के साथ। वही shape, हर बार।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.intro: Internet पर हर webpage — Google, YouTube, तुम्हारे friend का page — under the hood HTML code है. Browser उस code को read करता है और page draw करता है. किसी भी webpage पर right-click करो और View Page Source pick करो खुद देखने के लिए. Chrome, Firefox, Safari, और Edge जैसे browsers अलग-अलग programs हैं, पर सब वही HTML read करते हैं — इसीलिए हम HTML को web STANDARD बोलते हैं. HTML खुद 1989 में Tim Berners-Lee ने CERN, science lab में, invent किया था. वही idea, वही shape, अब भी modern internet को चला रहा है.
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.progFilter: एक्सटेंशन फ़िल्टर करने के लिए ऊपर Programming टैब पर क्लिक करें। आपको HTML 5 एक्सटेंशन दिखेगा।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.selectHtml5: HTML 5 card पर click करो ताकि HTML extension तुम्हारे project में add हो जाए। यह तुम्हें web pages बनाने के लिए ज़रूरी सारे blocks देता है।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.tagAnatomy: Har HTML tag के तीन parts होते हैं — OPEN tag, CLOSE tag, और बीच में जो STUFF तुम डालते हो वो. जो blocks तुम use करोगे, वो उस stuff के around shape लेते हैं. देखो — हर C-shape block एक tag है जिसमें एक slot होता है जो अंदर है उसके लिए. Open ऊपर, close नीचे, और slot बीच में.
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.titleInBrowserTa: title tag special है। इसका text page पर nahi दिखता — यह window के सबसे ऊपर BROWSER TAB में दिखता है। इसी से तुम अपने page को नाम देते हो।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.uploadMode: Upload मोड में स्विच करने के लिए ऊपर दाईं ओर Upload टॉगल पर क्लिक करें। इससे दाईं ओर HTML और View टैब दिखेंगे।
gui.tutorials.htmlHello.viewTab: View tab पर click करो ताकि तुम्हारा web page browser में rendered दिखे। बधाई हो — तुमने अपना पहला HTML page बना लिया!
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.addHeading: Body के अंदर एक heading drag करो. Level को 1 पर रखो और type करो My Favourite Animal.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.altFallback: Alt text तब show होता है जब image load nahi हो पाती — connection टूट गया, गलत filename, कुछ भी। यह screen readers की भी मदद करता है ताकि वो उन लोगों को pictures describe कर सकें जो देख nahi सकते। जब सब कुछ perfectly काम कर रहा हो, तब भी alt invisibly अपना काम कर रहा है।
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.attributesConce: कुछ tags अपने OPEN tag पर ही extra information carry करते हैं — जैसे एक tag picture का web address और एक description carry करता है. इन extras को ATTRIBUTES कहते हैं — एक name और एक value, equals sign से जुड़े हुए. Block में हर एक के लिए एक slot होता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.dragImg: Body के अंदर, heading के नीचे, image block drag करो.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.recap: Image के दो parts हैं: source, जिसे src कहते हैं, जो बताता है picture कहाँ से आती है, और alt, जो उसे describe करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.selfClosing: जब तुम थोड़ी देर में img block उठाओगे, एक चीज़ different notice करना — यह STACK shape का है, C-shape नहीं. ज़्यादातर blocks (जैसे p, h1, div) के बीच में एक SLOT होता है content को nest करने के लिए. img में नहीं होता. ऐसा इसलिए क्योंकि img tag SELF-CLOSING है — वो अकेला खड़ा रहता है, अपनी सारी information attributes में रखता है (src और alt). दूसरे self-closing tags भी इसी तरह काम करते हैं: input (form box) और br (line break). वो content को WRAP नहीं करते — वो खुद ही content हैं. जब तुम्हें toolbox में कोई stack-shape block दिखे, यह hint है कि वो tag self-closing है.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.setAlt: Alt field पर click करो. Type करो A puppy looking at the camera. Alt picture को words में describe करता है. ये उन लोगों की help करता है जो image नहीं देख सकते.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.setSource: Image block के source field पर click करो, जिसे src कहते हैं. Text को इससे replace करो: h t t p s colon slash slash picsum dot photos slash i d slash two three seven slash four hundred slash three hundred.
gui.tutorials.htmlImages.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. क्या तुम्हें अपनी heading के नीचे puppy दिखता है?
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.about: Home page जैसा ही structure बनाओ — site title के साथ header, तीनों links के साथ nav, फिर एक section. इस बार section है "About Me" जिसका text है "I love exploring and building websites with CodeSkool."
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.contact: New Document पर फिर click करो. वही header और nav बनाओ. Section title: "Get in Touch". Paragraph: "Email me at: hello@example.com". contact.html के नाम से save करो.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.files: View Files button पर click करो. तुमको दिखेंगे index.html, about.html, और contact.html — तीन real pages तुम्हारे browser में store हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.header: Body के अंदर एक header drop करो. Header के अंदर, एक h1 drop करो जिसका text है "My Adventure Site" — ये title हर page पर appear होगा.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.nav: h1 के बाद, एक nav block drop करो. Nav के अंदर, तीन anchors add करो जिनके hrefs हैं index.html, about.html, contact.html — और texts हैं Home, About, Contact. अभी ये links कहीं नहीं जाते — हम हर page को save करके इसे fix करेंगे.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.newDoc: New Document button पर click करो — page-with-plus icon. HTML document pick करो. Editor एक fresh page के लिए clear हो जाता है. जो home page तुमने अभी save किया वो safely stored है — तुम उसे फिर देखोगे.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.recap: तुमने तीन pages बनाए, हर एक को अपने filename के साथ save किया, और menu links उनके बीच काम करते हैं. ये एक real multi-page website है — internet पर तुम जो भी site visit करते हो वो under the hood ठीक इसी तरह काम करती है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.saveAbout: Save पर click करो. Dialog "index2" suggest करता है क्योंकि index.html पहले से exist करता है. इसे "about" में change करो. Save पर click करो. अब तुम्हारे पास दो saved pages हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.saveIndex: Save button पर click करो. File name में "index" type करो. Save पर click करो. तुम्हारा home page अब index.html के नाम से stored है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.section: Header के बाद, एक section drop करो. इसके अंदर: एक h2 "Welcome!" और एक paragraph "This is my home page. Use the menu above to explore."
gui.tutorials.htmlLinkPages.view: index.html open करो और View tab पर click करो. Menu में About पर click करो — page तुम्हारे about page पर change हो जाता है. Contact पर click करो, Home पर click करो. तीन pages, real navigation, बिना internet के.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.about: ul के अंदर, पहले के नीचे एक और li drag करो. URL about.html के साथ एक link add करो और एक text block जिसमें About Us लिखा हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.addText: Link के अंदर एक text block drag करो — link के open और close के बीच. Type करो Wikipedia Adventure. ये वो words हैं जो तुम्हारा reader click करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.contact: ul के अंदर एक तीसरा li add करो, contact.html के लिए एक link के साथ और एक text block जिसमें Contact लिखा हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.dragAnchor: Body के अंदर, heading के नीचे, एक link block drag करो. Link block के top पर एक URL slot होता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.heading: Body के अंदर एक heading drag करो. Level 1 रखो और type करो My Favourite Sites.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.home: ul के अंदर एक li drag करो. li के अंदर एक link block drag करो, इसका URL index.html set करो, और link के अंदर एक text block रखो जिसमें Home लिखा हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.recap: Link के दो parts हैं — URL जहाँ वो जाता है, और text जिसे लोग click करते हैं. एक ul के अंदर बहुत सारे links रखो menu बनाने के लिए. Real websites इसी recipe को use करती हैं अपने pages को connect करने के लिए.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.setUrl: Link block के top पर URL field पर click करो और text को इससे replace करो: h t t p s colon slash slash en dot wikipedia dot org slash wiki slash Adventure.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.ul: अब चलो बहुत सारे links को एक menu में बदलते हैं. Body के अंदर, link के नीचे एक ul block drag करो. ul हर menu item को hold करेगा.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.urlAnatomy: जो URL तुमने type किया उसे देखो. एक web address के parts होते हैं — और एक बार तुम उन्हें जान लो, तो web addresses random feel नहीं होते. https:// है PROTOCOL — वो language जो browser server से बात करने के लिए use करता है. अगला part है DOMAIN — site का name (जैसे google.com या youtube.com). Slash के बाद आता है PATH — उस site पर तुम्हें कौन सा page चाहिए. कभी-कभी एक question mark और extra info होती है जिसको QUERY parameters कहते हैं. हर link जो तुम लिखोगे इसी तरह break down होता है. Parts जानने से तुम bad links spot कर सकते हो — wrong protocol, wrong domain, missing path — और working ones लिख सकते हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlLinks.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. तुमको top पर एक Wikipedia link दिखना चाहिए, और उसके नीचे Home, About Us, और Contact के साथ एक menu.
gui.tutorials.htmlLists.dragUl: Body के अंदर list block drag करो, जिसे ul कहते हैं. ये एक empty list बनाता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLists.li1: ul के अंदर एक list item block drag करो, जिसे li कहते हैं. li के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो Tent. हर li एक bullet बनता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLists.li2: पहले के नीचे, ul के अंदर ही, एक और li drag करो. li के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो Compass.
gui.tutorials.htmlLists.li3: ul के अंदर, बाकियों के नीचे, एक तीसरा li drag करो. इसके अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो Snacks.
gui.tutorials.htmlLists.recap: List के दो parts हैं: ul list को hold करता है, और हर li एक item है. Numbered lists के लिए ol use करो जब order matter करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlLists.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. क्या तुम्हें तीन bullets एक के नीचे एक stacked दिखते हैं?
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.btnCss: एक और selector drag करो. इसका text .btn में change करो. इसके अंदर, background-color को orange पर set करके add करो, color को white पर, और padding को 12px पर.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.cardCss: एक final selector drag करो. इसका text .card में change करो. इसके अंदर, padding को 20px पर set करके add करो और background-color को lavender पर.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.features: Hero के नीचे एक दूसरा section drag करो. इसकी class को features set करो. इसके अंदर, तीन div blocks drag करो हर एक की class card के साथ. हर card में, level 2 की एक heading और एक paragraph add करो — Innovate / Ideas into action, Create / With purpose, और Inspire / Empower others.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.featuresC: एक और selector drag करो. इसका text .features में change करो. इसके अंदर, display को flex पर set करके add करो, gap को 20px पर, और padding को 30px पर.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.featuresT: तुम्हारे page की shape अब एक tree जैसी है — ऊपर header, नीचे hero, अंदर features, फिर footer। हर section एक div है class के साथ ताकि हम next में style कर सकें। बड़ी sites सब इसी shape को follow करती हैं।
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.footer: Body के अंदर, features section के नीचे एक footer drag करो. Footer के अंदर एक paragraph drag करो और type करो Code today, change tomorrow.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.header: Body के अंदर एक header drag करो. Header के अंदर, एक nav block drag करो. Nav के अंदर, चार link blocks एक के बाद एक drag करो, अंदर text blocks के साथ जो Home, About, Projects, और Contact कहते हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.hero: Header के नीचे एक section drag करो. इसकी class को hero set करो. इसके अंदर, एक heading drag करो और type करो Create Your Future, फिर एक paragraph जिसमें Build today, change tomorrow लिखो. फिर एक link block drag करो, इसकी class को btn set करो, और अंदर एक text block रखो जिसमें Get Started लिखा हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.heroCss: Style के अंदर एक selector drag करो. इसका text .hero में change करो. इसके अंदर, background-color को mediumpurple पर set करके add करो, color को white पर, text-align को center पर, और padding को 60px पर.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.recap: तुम Hello Web से एक पूरी styled website तक आ गए. कोई भी site जो तुम बनाओगे वही recipe use करेगी — structure के लिए HTML, style के लिए CSS, और बाकी सब के लिए तुम्हारी imagination. तीसरी layer है JavaScript — ये BEHAVIOUR add करती है (buttons जो कुछ DO करते हैं, animations, games जो respond करती हैं). वो एक पूरा नया track है जब तुम ready हो. और जब तुम exactly देखना चाहो कि एक tag या property कैसे काम करती है, MDN (developer.mozilla.org) वो reference है जो हर web developer use करता है — bookmark कर लो.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.style: Style block को head के अंदर drag करो. इस site को shine करने का time आ गया है.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItHappen.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. ये तुम्हारा capstone है — एक real, modern-looking website navigation menu, hero, तीन feature cards, और एक footer के साथ.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.header: Body के अंदर एक header drag करो. Header की class को site-header set करो. इसके अंदर एक heading drag करो और type करो My Website.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.hero: Header के नीचे एक section drag करो. इसकी class को hero set करो. इसके अंदर, level 2 की एक heading drag करो और type करो Welcome! फिर एक link block drag करो, इसकी class को btn set करो, और अंदर एक text block रखो जिसमें See My Work लिखा हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.recap: site-header, hero, और btn जैसी classes labels हैं जो तुम HTML blocks पर लगाते हो. CSS उन labels को use करके तुम्हारी site के हर part को exactly वैसे style करता है जैसे तुम चाहते हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.styleBtn: एक तीसरा selector drag करो. इसका text .btn में change करो. इसके अंदर, background-color को orange पर set करके add करो, color को white पर, और padding को 10px पर.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.styleHeade: Style block को head के अंदर drag करो. Style के अंदर एक selector drag करो और इसका text .site-header में change करो. Selector के अंदर एक background-color block drag करो और इसे navy पर set करो. एक color block drag करो और इसे white पर set करो. एक padding block add करो जो 20px पर set हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.styleHero: पहले के नीचे एक और selector drag करो. इसका text .hero में change करो. इसके अंदर, एक text-align block add करो जो center पर set हो और एक padding block जो 40px पर set हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlMakeItYours.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. क्या तुमको navy header, centred welcome message, और orange button दिखता है?
gui.tutorials.htmlParagraphs.addP1: Body में एक paragraph drag करो और type करो I love adventures.
gui.tutorials.htmlParagraphs.addP2: पहले के नीचे, body के अंदर ही, एक और paragraph drag करो. Type करो I want to explore new places.
gui.tutorials.htmlParagraphs.addP3: दूसरे के नीचे, body के अंदर ही, एक और paragraph add करो. Type करो Tomorrow I will start my journey.
gui.tutorials.htmlParagraphs.recap: हर paragraph writing का एक block है. Browser उनके बीच space add करता है ताकि तुम्हारा text आसानी से पढ़ा जा सके. हर idea के लिए एक paragraph use करो.
gui.tutorials.htmlParagraphs.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. Page देखो — क्या तुम्हारे sentences blocks में separated हैं? क्या उनके बीच space दिखता है?
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.clickSave: ऊपर toolbar में, Save button पर click करो — floppy-disk icon. या Ctrl+S press करो. एक naming dialog खुलता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.dialog: File name में "index" type करो. इसके बगल वाला dropdown पहले से .html पर set है. Save पर click करो. तुम्हारा page अब browser में index.html के नाम से stored है.
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.heading: Body के अंदर एक heading drop करो और type करो "Hello from my own webpage!".
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.paragraph: Heading के बाद एक paragraph add करो: "I built this with CodeSkool and saved it."
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.recap: तुमने एक page बनाया, उसे index.html के नाम से save किया, और file list open करके उसे देखा. Saved pages real .html files हैं तुम्हारे browser के अंदर — अगले tutorial के लिए ready जहाँ तुम कई pages को link करोगे.
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.title: Head के अंदर एक title block drop करो. Type करो "My First Saved Page" — ये नाम browser tab पर दिखता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlSavePage.viewFiles: View Files button पर click करो — toolbar में list icon. तुमको अपनी saved files में index.html दिखेगा. कभी भी इस page पर वापस आ सकते हो.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.footer: Body के अंदर, last section के नीचे एक footer drag करो. इसके अंदर एक paragraph drag करो और type करो Built with care, 2026.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.header: Body के अंदर एक header drag करो. Header के अंदर, एक heading drag करो और type करो My Awesome Website. Header हर page के top पर बैठता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.labelledRooms: अब तक हम <div> use कर रहे थे — एक generic box जिसको तुम खुद label करते हो. अब तुम मिलोगे semantic tags से — <header>, <nav>, <section>, <footer>. ये वही काम करते हैं जो div करता है, लेकिन इनके NAMES helpers को (search engines, screen readers) बताते हैं कि तुम्हारे page का हर part क्या है. जैसे घर के rooms — div सिर्फ "a room" है, लेकिन <kitchen> सबको बता देता है कि वो किसलिए है.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.nav: Heading के नीचे, header के अंदर ही, एक nav block drag करो. Nav के अंदर, तीन link blocks एक के बाद एक drag करो अंदर text blocks के साथ जो Home, About, और Contact कहते हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.recap: Header, nav, section, और footer को semantic tags कहते हैं क्योंकि उनके नाम बताते हैं अंदर क्या है. ये browsers, search engines, और screen readers की help करते हैं तुम्हारे page को समझने में.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. क्या तुमको top पर एक header, एक navigation menu, middle में दो sections, और bottom पर एक footer दिखता है?
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.welcome: Body के अंदर, header के नीचे एक section block drag करो. Section के अंदर, level 2 की एक heading drag करो और type करो Welcome! फिर एक paragraph add करो जो कहता है This is my first real-looking website.
gui.tutorials.htmlSections.whatIDo: पहले के नीचे एक और section drag करो. अंदर, level 2 की एक heading drag करो और type करो What I Do, फिर एक paragraph जो कहता है I build websites that help people.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.dataCells: दूसरे tr के अंदर एक td block drag करो. इसके अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो Pingu. फिर इसके बगल में एक और td drag करो, अंदर एक text block drag करो, और type करो Penguin. td का मतलब है table data cell.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.dataRow: Header row के नीचे, table के अंदर ही, एक और tr block drag करो. ये data की एक row hold करेगा.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.dragTable: Body के अंदर table block drag करो. Table data को rows और columns में hold करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.headerCells: tr के अंदर एक th block drag करो. th के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो Name. फिर उसी tr के अंदर इसके बगल में एक और th drag करो, अंदर एक text block drag करो, और type करो Species. th का मतलब है table header cell.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.headerRow: Table के अंदर एक tr block drag करो. tr का मतलब है table row — ये header row होगी.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.recap: Table rows से बनती है. हर tr एक row है. हर row के अंदर, th एक header cell है और td एक data cell. Tables data को rows और columns में दिखाती हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlTables.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. क्या तुमको data की दो rows और दो columns दिखती हैं?
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.bold: Body के अंदर, heading के नीचे b block drag करो. b के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो This is bold text. b के अंदर जो भी बैठता है वो page पर thicker हो जाता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.combine: एक paragraph drag करो underlined text के नीचे. Paragraph के अंदर एक b block drag करो. b के अंदर एक i block drag करो. i के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो "This is bold AND italic." Tags के अंदर tags COMBINE होते हैं — page उन्हीं words पर दोनों effects दिखाता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.heading: Body के अंदर एक heading drag करो. Level 1 रखो और type करो Bold, Italic, Underline.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.inlineV: एक चीज़ है जो शायद तुमने notice की हो. तुम्हारा bold text और italic text दूसरे words के साथ SAME LINE पर बैठे थे. लेकिन तुम्हारे paragraphs और headings हर एक NEW line से शुरू हुए. ऐसा क्यों? HTML elements दो flavours में आते हैं. INLINE elements (जैसे b, i, u, a) words के flow में बैठते हैं extra colour या emphasis की तरह — वो line को break नहीं करते. BLOCK elements (जैसे p, h1, div) हर एक पूरी line खुद ले लेते हैं — अगली चीज़ को उनके नीचे से ही start होना पड़ता है. ज़्यादातर बार तुम्हें इसके बारे में सोचना नहीं पड़ता; browser automatically सही काम कर देता है. बस यह जान लो कि difference है — इससे समझ आता है क्यों कुछ चीज़ें line में wrap हो जाती हैं और कुछ नीचे jump कर जाती हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.italic: Body के अंदर, b block के नीचे ही, एक i block drag करो. i के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो This is italic text. Italic letters को slant करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.recap: Bold के लिए b use करो, italic के लिए i, और underlined के लिए u. strong और em tags भी हैं जो b और i की तरह दिखते हैं पर screen readers को बताते हैं कि ये words important हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.slotIns: हर C-shape block के बीच में एक SLOT होता है — वो content slot है. जो कुछ भी तुम उस slot में डालते हो, वो open tag और close tag के बीच का STUFF बन जाता है. आज हम देखेंगे कि क्या होता है जब blocks दूसरे blocks के slots के अंदर जाते हैं. इसको nesting कहते हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.tripleS: अब i के अंदर एक u को nest करो. वही words अब bold, italic, और underlined बन जाते हैं. तुम जितना deeper nest करोगे, उतने ज़्यादा effects pile होते जाएंगे.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.underli: Body के अंदर, i block के नीचे ही, एक u block drag करो. u के अंदर एक text block drag करो और type करो This is underlined text. u block अंदर जो भी हो उसके नीचे एक line draw करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlTextFormatting.viewTab: View tab पर click करो. क्या तुमको एक bold line, एक italic line, और एक underlined line दिखती है?
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.alt: alt field में एक short description type करो — "A photo of my pet". ये तब दिखता है जब picture load नहीं हो पाती और screen readers को image describe करने में help करता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.dragImg: Heading के बाद एक img block snap करो. अभी ये एक placeholder picture पर point करता है — चलो इसे तुम्हारी अपनी picture से swap करते हैं.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.heading: Body के अंदर एक heading drop करो और type करो "My Pet" — वो तुम्हारी photo के ऊपर का title है.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.pickFile: अपने computer से कोई भी photo pick करो — तुम्हारा pet, तुम्हारा toy, एक drawing — और confirm करो. CodeSkool इसे तुम्हारे project में save कर देता है.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.recap: तुमने एक image upload किया, उसका नाम रखा, उसे img src में use किया, और अपने page पर देखा. कोई भी चीज़ जिसकी तुम photo ले सकते हो अब तुम्हारे बनाए webpage पर जा सकती है.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.setSrc: img block में, वो filename type करो जो तुमने अभी upload किया (जैसे my-pet.jpg) src field में. Picture तुम्हारी upload की हुई photo पर point करती है अब.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.twoAddress: तुमने अब तक दो तरह के address देख लिए हैं. Image tutorial में एक लंबा web address use हुआ था जो https:// से start होता है — वो internet पर कहीं से भी काम करता है. इस बार तुमने सिर्फ my-pet.jpg type किया — यह एक SHORT address है जो सिर्फ इसलिए काम करता है क्योंकि file तुम्हारे page के SAME folder में है. दूर की pictures के लिए लंबे addresses, और जो files तुमने यहीं upload की हैं उनके लिए short addresses.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.uploadClic: Toolbar में ऊपर एक Upload Image button है — picture और plus वाला icon. इस पर click करो. तुम्हारे computer का file picker खुलेगा.
gui.tutorials.htmlUploadImage.view: View tab पर click करो. तुम्हारी actual picture तुम्हारे webpage पर है. वो तुम्हारे अपने computer से एक real photo है browser के अंदर rendered.
gui.tutorials.html_comments.boilerplate: तुम्हारे workspace में पहले से html, head, और body blocks हैं — हर webpage इसी तरह start होती है (तुमने ये Hello Web tutorial में बनाया था). आज हम सिर्फ उस पर focus करेंगे जो हम body के INSIDE add करते हैं.
gui.tutorials.html_css_box.boilerplate: तुम्हारे workspace में पहले से html, head, और body blocks हैं — हर webpage इसी तरह start होती है (तुमने ये Hello Web tutorial में बनाया था). आज हम सिर्फ उस पर focus करेंगे जो हम body के INSIDE add करते हैं.

gui.tutorials.html_upload_image.boilerpl: तुम्हारे workspace में पहले से html, head, और body blocks हैं — हर webpage इसी तरह start होती है (तुमने ये Hello Web tutorial में बनाया था). आज हम सिर्फ उस पर focus करेंगे जो हम body के INSIDE add करते हैं.
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepChangeCostumes: next costume block जोड़ो ताकि costumes बदल-बदल कर तुम्हारा sprite animate हो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepChooseAnotherB: अपनी world में नई scene बनाने के लिए एक और backdrop जोड़ो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepChooseAnotherS: अपनी world को और मज़ेदार बनाने के लिए एक और sprite जोड़ो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepChooseBackdrop: Choose a Backdrop button पर click करो और अपनी world की scene set करने के लिए कोई भी backdrop चुनो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepChooseSound: library से कोई sound चुनो और अपने code में play sound block जोड़ो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepChooseSprite: Choose a Sprite button पर click करो और अपनी world में जोड़ने के लिए कोई भी sprite चुनो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepClickGreenFlag: green flag पर click करके देखो कि तुम्हारा character तुम्हारा typed message बोल रहा है।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepFlyAround: when space key pressed block और glide block जोड़ो ताकि space दबाने पर तुम्हारा sprite उड़ने लगे।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepGlideToPoint: अपने sprite को किसी specific जगह तक glide कराने के लिए glide to x y block use करो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepGrowShrink: अपने sprite को बड़ा-छोटा करने के लिए change size block use करो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepLeftRight: इस sprite को left और right move कराने के लिए when key pressed के साथ change x block use करो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepRecordASound: Sounds tab पर click करो और अपनी world में बजाने के लिए कोई sound जोड़ो या record करो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepSwitchBackdrop: अलग-अलग scenes के बीच बदलने के लिए switch backdrop block use करो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepTypeWhatYouWan: say block पर click करो और जो कुछ तुम चाहते हो कि character बोले वो type कर दो।
gui.tutorials.imagine.stepUpDown: इस sprite को ऊपर-नीचे move कराने के लिए when key pressed के साथ change y block use करो।
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.andOperator: Drag an if block. Slot — age greater-than ten AND hasPass. Body — log Allowed. Both halves must be true. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.combining: Real checks combine all three — age greater-than eighteen AND hasPass OR isVip. Parentheses group them when you mix, just like maths.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.flipRerun: Change hasPass to false. Click Run. Buy a pass first now prints. NOT flipped false into true.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.notOperator: Add a third if. Slot — exclamation hasPass. That is NOT. It flips true to false. Body — log Buy a pass first. hasPass is true, so NOT hasPass is false. Nothing prints.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.oneCheckRare: You know and, or, and not from Scratch's boolean blocks. JavaScript spells them differently. Two ampersands for AND. Two pipes for OR. An exclamation for NOT. Same logic, new symbols.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. Allowed appears. Both halves were true.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.orOperator: Add a second if. Slot — age greater-than eighteen OR hasPass. Body — log Welcome. Either half true is enough. Click Run. The age check is false, but hasPass is true. Welcome prints.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.setupVars: Drag a let block. Name it age, set to fifteen. Another let. Name it hasPass, set to true — no quotes. true and false are JavaScript's two boolean values.
gui.tutorials.jsAndOrNot.truthTables: AND — both true. OR — either true. NOT — flip. Same rules you know from Scratch.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.callback: Here is the new idea. The little block you handed to forEach is called a CALLBACK — a function you hand to another function so it can call you back. forEach calls your callback once per item. Callbacks are everywhere in JavaScript — events, timers, fetch, all of them use callbacks.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.filter: Set bigOnes to numbers dot filter, keep if n is greater than two. Log bigOnes. Click Run. You see three, four, five — only the items the callback returned true for.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.forEach: Drag a numbers dot forEach block. Use n as the item name. Inside, log n. forEach is like for-of but you pass in a little function. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.map: Set doubled to numbers dot map with n times two. Log doubled. Click Run. You see two, four, six, eight, ten — a brand-new array. map turns each item into a new one and gives you the new array.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. Each number prints on its own line. forEach walked through every item for you.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.setupNumber: Make an array called numbers with one, two, three, four, five.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.shapeOfMode: Real JS code reads like English with these. Take the users. filter the active ones. map their names. log them. Chain them together and a five-line problem fits on one line.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.takeFunctio: Scratch lists do not have anything like these. Arrays come with tools that take a small function and run it on every item. The three big ones — forEach, map, and filter. They are the heart of modern JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsArrayMethods.threeToolsJ: forEach visits each item. map transforms each into a new one. filter keeps only items that pass a test. Each takes a callback.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.addPush: Call fruits dot push with kiwi. That sticks kiwi on the end — same as Scratch's add to list. Log fruits again. Four items now.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.howManyLength: Log fruits dot length. Click Run. The answer is three. dot length is JavaScript's version of Scratch's length-of-list.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.indexFromZero: Big surprise. Scratch lists start at position ONE — item one is the first thing. JavaScript arrays start at ZERO. The first item is fruits zero. The second is fruits one. The third is fruits two. This trips up every Scratch kid for the first week.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.makeArray: Make an array called fruits with three items — apple, banana, mango. Each fruit goes in quotes.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.oneNameMany: You already use lists in Scratch — add things to list, item one of list, length of list. JavaScript calls them ARRAYS. Same idea, different word, with a few surprises.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. The whole array prints — brackets, commas, all of it.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.printWhole: Drop a log to console block. Pass it fruits. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.readByIndex: Log fruits at index zero. Then fruits at index two. Click Run. You see apple and mango — first and third items.
gui.tutorials.jsArrays.threePowers: Same three list jobs you know from Scratch — read by index, count, add — just spelled fruits zero, dot length, dot push. Remember zero-based.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.arrowAdd: Drag an arrow function block. Name it add. Inputs are a and b. Expression is a plus b. The arrow reads as gives-back. a and b give back a plus b.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.bothShape: Arrow for short callbacks. Classic function for bigger logic with a name. You will see both everywhere in real code.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.noCurly: When the body is one expression, you skip the return word — JavaScript adds it for you. Less typing, same result.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.openConso: Click the Console button next to Run. You see sixteen.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.shorterSh: JavaScript has a shorter way to write functions. Modern JS uses these constantly — especially for callbacks. The shape uses an arrow — equals greater-than. Same idea — input in, value out — but tighter.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.trySquare: Make an arrow function called square. Input is x. Expression is x times x. Log square called with four. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsArrowFunctions.withCurly: When the body needs many lines, use the arrow function with a body block. Make greet — input is name, body logs Hi space joined with name.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.alwaysText: One warning — whatever the user types comes back as TEXT. Even if they type the digit five, you get the text five, not the number. The Numbers tutorial teaches the fix.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.askName: Ek let block ko script hat ke neeche drag karo. Iska naam name rakho. Value slot ke liye, Dialogs section mein ask user block dhundo aur use value slot ke ANDAR drop karo. ask user ka prompt set karo What is your name. Ye Scratch se alag hai — answer seedha variable ki value ke roop mein, ek hi step mein wapas aati hai. Koi separate answer reporter nahi.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.askStoreUse: Pucho. Answer ko variable mein store karo. Variable ko baad mein use karo. Wahi shape jo tumne Scratch mein use kiya tha, JavaScript mein likha hua. Agle tutorial mein tum sikhoge ki JavaScript numbers aur arithmetic kaise handle karta hai, aur kyon ask user text return karna kabhi kabhi surprise create karta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.askTheirName: Drag a let block. Name it name. Set the value to an ask user block that asks What is your name. Different from Scratch — the answer goes straight into the variable, not into a separate answer reporter.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.concatenation: Strings ko plus sign se jodne ko concatenation kehte hain — end-to-end join ke liye ek lambi term. JavaScript do text pieces ke beech plus sign dekhta hai, unhe jodta hai, aur tumhe ek combined string wapas deta hai. Ab se tum lagbhag har program mein concatenation use karoge.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.dragHat: JavaScript category kholo aur script hat ko empty workspace mein drag karo.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.greet: Ek log to console block ko let ke neeche drag karo. Message slot ke andar, ek join banao: literal text Hello-comma-space — wo to quotes mein hai kyunki wo fixed text hai — plus variable name bina quotes ke kyunki hum uski value chahte hain. JavaScript mein plus sign do strings ko jodta hai. Code panel ab dikhata hai console.log quote-open Hello-comma-space quote-close plus name bracket-close semicolon.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.greetThem: Drag a log to console block. Inside, join Hello comma space — in quotes — with the variable name, no quotes. The plus sign sticks two strings together — JavaScript's version of Scratch's join block.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.greetingShown: Wahaan hai — Hello, phir tumhara naam. JavaScript ne fixed text Hello-comma-space liya, name variable ki value end mein chipkayi, aur pura cheez ek hi baar mein print kar diya.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.openConsole: Run ke bagal mein greater-than underscore button par click karo. Console pane neeche right mein dikhega.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.returnsText: Ek important warning. User jo bhi type kare, wo TEXT ke roop mein wapas aati hai — chahe wo digits hi kyon na type kare. Agar kid five type karta hai, tumhe text five milta hai, number five nahi. Ye matter karta hai jab tum uss par maths karne ki koshish karte ho. Aane wala Numbers tutorial exactly dikhayega kab ye bite karta hai aur kaise fix karna hai.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.run: Run par click karo. Preview ke upar ek popup aata hai jo puchhta hai What is your name. Apna naam type karo aur OK click karo.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.runIt: Click Run. The popup appears over the preview. Type a name. Click OK.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.talksBack: Scratch mein tum ask aur wait block use karte ho user se answer lene ke liye. JavaScript mein prompt hai — ek popup jo question puchhta hai aur kid ke type karne ka wait karta hai. Yahaan ka block ask user kehlata hai. Same idea, browser style mein. Is tutorial mein tum naam puchhoge aur phir us person ko naam se greet karoge.
gui.tutorials.jsAskTheUser.whatIsGluing: Sticking strings with the plus sign is called concatenation — a long word for end-to-end join. JavaScript hands back one combined string.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.bumpCount: Inside the click body, drag a change by block. Variable is count. Amount is one. Just like Scratch's change variable by.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.clickScript: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Drag the script hat.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.grabBoth: A const named btn with find element by id and the text go. A const named counter with find element by id and the text counter.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.letCount: Drag a let block. Name it count. Value is zero. This remembers how many clicks have happened.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.listenerShap: Drag a when click on block. Element is btn. Event is click. The blocks inside the C shape run every time the button is clicked — like a Scratch when this sprite clicked hat block, but for any HTML element.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.mostUsedLine: Scratch has hat blocks like when this sprite clicked. JavaScript does the same with addEventListener. The when click on block is JS's way of saying — when this button is clicked, run these blocks. The most-used pattern on the web.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.pageReady: The page sprite already has a button with id go and text Click me, and a p with id counter and text Clicks zero. You edit the script sprite to wire the click.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.readLikeSent: Read the line aloud — when btn is clicked, run these blocks. Pros read code this way to spot bugs.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.saveAndClick: Click Save. Default name is script. Click Save. Then View Files, open index.html. Click the button. The counter goes up. Click again. Up again.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.showCount: Still inside the click body. Drag a set text of block. Element is counter. Use a join to glue Clicks space and the count variable. The paragraph updates on every click.
gui.tutorials.jsButtonClick.threeStepPat: Grab the element. Listen for an event. Run blocks. This three-step pattern lives on every interactive site — every like button, every menu, every search box.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.advance: After the if, still inside the click body. Set text of scoreEl to a join of Score, a space, and score. Change index by one. Then call showQuestion. The next question appears.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.checkAnswer: Inside the click body. An if block. Condition — the value of aEl made lowercase triple-equals the answer at position index made lowercase. If true, change score by one. Lowercase makes the check forgiving.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.clickScript: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Drag the script hat.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.defineShow: Drag a function block. Name it showQuestion. No parameters. It draws the current question on the page.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.drawCurrent: After the if, still inside showQuestion. A set text of block. Element is qEl. Text is the question text at position index from the questions array. Then a set text of block. Element is aEl. Text is empty. The input clears between questions.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.endGuard: Inside showQuestion. An if block. Condition — index is not less-than the length of questions. Inside — set text of qEl to Quiz over. Set style display of aEl to none. Same for nextBtn. Then a return. return exits the function early.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.firstCall: Outside the listener, at the bottom of the script, add one call to showQuestion. That draws the first question when the page loads. Without it, the page starts blank.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.listAppShap: A list of data. A function that draws one item. A button that advances. That shape powers Twitter showing tweets, Spotify showing songs, every comment thread on every site. Capstone done — you can now build real JavaScript apps.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.pageReady: The page sprite already has a p with id q, an input with id a, a button with id next and text Next, and a p with id score and text Score zero. You edit the script sprite to wire the quiz.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.puttingToge: Capstone day. Today you build a real quiz. Arrays, functions, the DOM, click events — all wired together. By the end you have a working quiz that scores the player.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.questionsLi: A const named questions. Inside, an array of three objects. Each object has a question and an answer property. Two plus two — four. Capital of France — Paris. Legs on a spider — eight. Arrays of objects is how real apps store data.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.saveAndPlay: Click Save. Default name is script. Click Save. Then View Files, open index.html. Type an answer. Click Next. The score updates and the next question appears. Three rounds, then Quiz over.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.stateRefs: A let named index with value zero. A let named score with value zero. Four const blocks — qEl, aEl, nextBtn, scoreEl. Each uses find element by id with the matching id.
gui.tutorials.jsCapstoneQuiz.wireNext: Drag a when click on block. Element is nextBtn. Event is click. The body runs on every Next click.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.addClass: Add a const named message with find element by id and the text message. Drag a class on element block. Action is add. Class name is highlight. Element is message.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.camelCase: Small gotcha. CSS uses dashes — font-size, background-color. JavaScript cannot have dashes in names, so they become fontSize, backgroundColor — camelCase. The browser still understands.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.classWins: One or two inline styles is fine. For many styles, write them once in a CSS class and just add the class from JavaScript. Pros pick class almost every time.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.clickScript: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Empty workspace ready.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.clickStyle: Click the style sprite. The workspace switches to CSS blocks. Same project — just a different file.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.defineHighli: Drop a rule for the highlight class. Background yellow, padding ten pixels. The CSS rule waits in style.css for the JS to add the class.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.grabTitle: Drag the script hat. Add a const named title with find element by id and the text title.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.restyleLive: In Scratch you change a sprite's costume or colour to restyle it live. On a webpage you change CSS. Two ways from JavaScript — set a single style, or add a CSS class. Both work.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.saveAndView: Click Save on the style sprite. Default name is style, extension .css. Click Save. Switch back to script and Save it too. Then View Files, open index.html. The h1 is tomato and big, and the message paragraph glows yellow.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.setColor: Drag a set style block. Property is color. Element is title. Value is tomato. Behind the scenes this is title dot style dot color equals tomato.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.setFontSize: Add another set style block. Property is fontSize. Element is title. Value is 48px. The heading will grow when you save and open the page.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.threeSprites: Today the sprite selector has three. page already has the h1 with id title and the p with id message and a link to style.css. style is the CSS sprite. script is the JS sprite. You edit script first, then style.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeStyle.twoWays: set style for quick tweaks. class on element for organised styling. Both update the page live.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.clickScript: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Empty workspace ready for code.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.grabAndChange: Last chapter you grabbed an element. Now you change what is inside it — the JS lines textContent and innerHTML. The page updates instantly.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.grabTitle: Drag the script hat. Add a const named title with find element by id and the text title.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.pageNowAlive: Your first visible JavaScript effect on a real page. Like changing a sprite's say bubble, but on a webpage instead of the Scratch stage.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.sameTwoSprite: Same shape as last time. The page sprite already has the h1 with id title and the p with id message. You edit the script sprite to change them from JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.saveAndView: Click Save. Default name is script. Click Save. Then View Files, open index.html. The h1 now reads Changed by JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.seeBold: Save the script. Reopen index.html. The word bold appears in actual bold. set HTML treats the value as HTML and parses the tags inside.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.setHtml: Add a const named message with find element by id and the text message. Drag a set HTML of block — this is dot innerHTML in real JS. Element is message. Type Now with a real b tag around the word bold. Open-angle-bracket b close-angle-bracket bold open-angle-bracket slash b close-angle-bracket.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.setText: Drag a set text of block. Element is title. Text is Changed by JavaScript. Behind the scenes this is title dot textContent equals Changed by JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsChangeText.textVsHtml: set text treats the value as plain text. set HTML reads tags inside the value. Pick set text by default — set HTML only when you really mean to inject markup.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.belowFifty: Add a third if. Slot — score less-than fifty. Body — log Fail. Click Run, type forty-five. Now Fail prints.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.openConsole: Run ke baju mein >_ button click karo. Console pane khulta hai. Perfect print hota hai. Phir chalao aur koi aur number type karo — kuch print nahi hoga, kyunki condition false thi.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.passMark: Add a second if. Slot — score greater-than-or-equal fifty. Body — log Pass. Click Run, type seventy — Pass. Type forty-five — nothing.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.perfectScore: Drag an if block. Inside the slot, score triple-equals one hundred. Inside the body, log Perfect. Click Run. Type one hundred.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.setupScore: Drag a let block. Name it score. Wrap a Number block around an ask user block that asks Score. We will run three checks against the answer.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.sixTools: Tum Scratch ke greater-than, less-than aur equals jaante ho. JavaScript mein kul chhe comparison operators hain — greater-than, less-than, greater-than-or-equal, less-than-or-equal, equal ke liye triple-equals, aur not-equal ke liye exclamation-equals. Har comparison true ya false return karta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.sixToolsOneT: Six comparisons, every one returns true or false. Same shape as the boolean blocks you used in Scratch's if blocks — just spelled in JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsComparisons.whyThreeEqua: JavaScript also has double-equals. But double-equals is loose — it says the text five and the number five are equal. Triple-equals is strict — same value AND same type, or false. Always use triple-equals. The pros do.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.areaShown: Console seventy-eight point five print karta hai. Yeh paanch radius wale circle ka area hai — pi multiply paanch multiply paanch. JavaScript ne arithmetic kiya, variables ne apni values rakhi, aur answer wahaan hai.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.defaultConst: Real JavaScript programmers ki ek aadat: by default const use karo. Sirf tab let par switch karo jab tum sach mein variable ko baad mein reassign karne wale ho. Values ko default mein lock karna bugs ki ek poori category ko jaldi pakad leta hai. Agle tutorial mein hum prompt use karke user se poochenge.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.defaultToCons: Real JavaScript uses const by default. Reach for let only when you plan to reassign. Locking things down catches bugs early.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.dragHat: Flyout mein JavaScript category kholo aur script hat block ko empty workspace mein drag karo. Har JavaScript program is hat se shuru hota hai.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.logArea: log to console block drag karke let ke neeche snap karo. Message slot mein formula banao pi multiply radius multiply radius — yeh circle ka area hai. Operators category se multiply operator drop karo, aur Variables section se pi aur radius variables grab karo. Kahin bhi quotes nahi — humein calculated number chahiye, literal text nahi.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.makeConstant: Drag a const block. Name it pi. Set it to three point one four. JavaScript now refuses any attempt to change pi.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.normalRadius: Radius can change for different circle sizes. Drag a let block, name it radius, set it to five.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.openConsole: Run ke baju mein >_ button par click karo. Console pane Console title ke saath bottom-right corner mein aata hai.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.pi: Flyout ke Variables section mein const block dhoondho. Use script hat ke neeche drag karo. Slot ka naam pi rakho aur value teen point ek char set karo. Right side mein Code panel const pi equals teen point ek char semicolon dikhata hai. JavaScript ab kisi bhi line ko refuse karega jo pi change karne ki koshish kare.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.radius: Radius alag-alag circle sizes ke liye change ho sakti hai, isliye yeh let mein jati hai. Const ke neeche let block drag karo, naam radius rakho, aur value paanch set karo. Code panel ab do lines dikhata hai: const pi equals teen point ek char; let radius equals paanch. Ek locked, ek changeable.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.redError: Console ab red error dikhata hai: assignment to constant variable. Yeh const ki safety hai. radius abhi bhi change ho sakti hai kyunki yeh let hai, par pi locked hai. Agar tum kabhi real program likhoge jisme ek variable fixed value honi chahiye, toh const guarantee karta hai ki tumhare code mein aur kuch us promise ko tod nahi sakta.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.run: Top-right corner mein Run button par click karo. View pane khali rehta hai — answer console mein jata hai.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.seeError: Open the console. A red error appears — assignment to constant variable. That is the safety const gives you. radius can still change. pi is locked.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.someNever: Scratch mein har variable kabhi bhi change ho sakta hai. JavaScript kuch ko lock karne deta hai. Jo values change karne wala hai usske liye let use karo. Jo values kabhi change nahi honi chahiye unke liye const use karo. Values lock karne se tum galti se kuch aisa change nahi karoge jo same rehna chahiye tha.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.tryBreak: Ab dekhte hain const kis cheez se bachata hai. Ek assign block drag karo — woh jo variable ko nayi value deta hai. pi ko char set karo. Hum jaan-boojhkar const change karne ki koshish kar rahe hain. Run par click karo.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.tryToBreak: Add a line that tries to set pi to four. Click Run. JavaScript will refuse.
gui.tutorials.jsConstVsLet.useTogether: Drag a log to console block. Inside it, pi times radius times radius — the area of a circle. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.appendIt: Drag an append to block. Child is li. Parent is list. Behind the scenes — list dot appendChild. Now the new li attaches to the ul, and the browser paints it instantly.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.clearInpu: Still inside the click body. Add a set text of block. Element is itemInput. Text is empty. That clears the input so the user can type the next item.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.clickScri: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Drag the script hat.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.createLi: Inside the click body, add a const named li. Value is create element with the tag li. Behind the scenes — document dot createElement. The new tag exists in memory only. It is NOT on the page yet.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.fillLi: Drag a set text of block. Element is li. Value is a get value of block with itemInput inside. That reads what the user typed into the input.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.grabThree: Three const blocks. itemInput with find element by id and the text itemInput. addBtn with the text add. list with the text list.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.growPage: Scratch has clone — make a new sprite at runtime. JavaScript can do the same to HTML — create brand-new elements from code and stick them on the page. Today you build a to-do list.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.pageReady: The page sprite already has an input with id itemInput, a button with id add, and an empty ul with id list. You edit the script sprite to wire them up.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.saveAndTr: Click Save. Default name is script. Click Save. Then View Files, open index.html. Type Apples. Click Add. The word appears as a list item. Type Bananas. Click Add. The list grows.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.threeStep: Create element. Set its text. Append. Three steps and you built a working to-do list. The same shape powers every comment thread and every list app.
gui.tutorials.jsCreateElements.wireClick: Drag a when click on block. Element is addBtn. Event is click. Body runs on every click.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.addBody: Inside the for, drop a log to console block. Print Round space joined with i. i is the loop counter you can read inside the body — Scratch's repeat block does not give you that.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.forShape: Drag a for block. Four slots — name, start, end, step. Use i, from one, to five, step one. i is the traditional name for a loop counter.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.forVsWhile: for when you know the count — like Scratch's repeat. while when the end depends on a condition — like Scratch's repeat-until.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.mostUsed: The for loop is the workhorse loop in JavaScript. Counting up, counting down, walking through a list.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. You see Round one through Round five.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.plusPlus: Step is how much i changes each round. One adds one. Two skips every other number. Minus one counts down. Scratch's repeat block can only count up by one — JS lets you pick.
gui.tutorials.jsForLoop.threeJobs: You know repeat ten times from Scratch. JavaScript's for loop is the same idea but you write three things on one line — start the counter, the check, and how to step. It is the most-used loop in JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.bodyLikeEach: Inside the body, log I like space joined with f. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.cleanerLoops: for-of is the cleanest way to walk an array in JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.forOfShape: Drag a for-of block. Use f as the name, fruits as the array. Each round, f holds one fruit.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.forOfVsClassic: for-of when you just need each item. Classic for when you also need the position number.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.madeForArrays: In Scratch you walk a list with a repeat plus item-i-of-list. JavaScript has a shortcut — the for-of loop. It hands you each item one by one. No counter, no index, no length check.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.nameIsYours: We used f for fruit. Pick whatever name reads well — fruit, item, thing.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. Four lines — I like apple, banana, mango, kiwi. One per item.
gui.tutorials.jsForOfLoop.setupArray: Make an array called fruits with four items — apple, banana, mango, kiwi.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctions.callIt: Drag a call block for greet — three times in a row.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctions.defineFunction: Drag a function block. Name it greet. Leave the inputs empty. Inside the body, log Hello with an exclamation mark. Just like Scratch's define-greet hat block.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctions.definingNotRun: Click Run. Nothing prints. You defined the function but never called it. Same as Scratch — a define hat block on its own does nothing until something calls it.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctions.nameForChunk: You already use Make-a-Block in Scratch to wrap reusable code. JavaScript calls these FUNCTIONS. Same idea — define once, call many times — different word.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctions.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. Hello prints three times. Three calls, one definition.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctions.wrapRepetition: Same rule as Scratch — anything you write twice belongs in a function. Real programs are mostly small functions calling other functions.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.callStor: Set result to add called with five and three. Log result. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.function: Drag a function block. Name it add. Inputs are a and b. Inside the body, return a plus b. The return keyword sends the value back to whoever called add.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.inputsOu: In Scratch, Make-a-Block has parameters and reporter blocks can report a value. JavaScript has the same — INPUTS called parameters, and the keyword RETURN to send a value back.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.openCons: Click the Console button next to Run. You see eight. The function returned eight, and you stored it.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.paramete: a and b are placeholders — same as Scratch Make-a-Block parameters. When you call add with five and three, a becomes five, b becomes three, and the answer is eight.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.paramsIn: Parameters in. return out. Same shape as a Scratch reporter Make-a-Block, written in JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.passAnyw: A returned value can go anywhere a value can go. Print it, store it, pass it to another function, use it in maths.
gui.tutorials.jsFunctionsReturn.useCallD: You do not have to store the answer. Log add called with ten and twenty. Click Run. add returns thirty, log prints thirty — all in one line.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.addExtension: Screen ke bottom-left mein Add Extension button par click karo. Yeh Extension Library kholega — wahin saare special block sets hain, jismein JavaScript blocks bhi hain jo hum use karne wale hain.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.alertPops: Run par phir se click karo. Is baar tumhara BROWSER khud page ke upar ek chhota dialog box pop-up karta hai jismein likha hai Hi there! — yahi alert hai. Pura page freeze ho jata hai jab tak tum OK click na karo. Yeh message dikhane ka LOUD tareeka hai — visitor ise ignore nahi kar sakta. Console message bhi print hua, par tum use tab tak nahi padh sakte jab tak alert close nahi karte. Chalo — alert par OK click karo.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.clickRun: Top-right corner mein Run button par click karo — wahi jo triangle play icon hai. Scratch ke flag ki tarah, yeh tumhara code run karta hai. Middle mein View pane dekho — woh KHALI rehta hai. Sahi baat hai — page par kuch nahi dikha. Pareshaan mat ho, tumhara message gaya hai — bas page par nahi, console par gaya hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.codePanel: Right side mein Code panel dekho. Yeh pehle se hi tumhare block se generate hua REAL JavaScript text dikha raha hai: console dot log open-bracket quote Hello JavaScript quote close-bracket semicolon. Yahi wahi line hai jo duniya bhar ke developers din mein hazaar baar type karte hain. Tumhara block use type karne ka shortcut hai. Code ke baju mein View tab bhi hai — woh actual webpage dikhata hai, par jab tak tum Run par click nahi karte tab tak blank rahta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.consoleResult: Yeh dekho — Hello JavaScript — tumhare khud ke program ne console mein print kiya. Tumne likha, tumne run kara, tumne kaam karte hue dekha. Ab message dikhane ka DUSRA tareeka try karein — woh loud wala jo visitor bhi dekhta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.dragAlert: Flyout mein JavaScript category phir se kholo aur show-alert block dhoondho. Use workspace mein drag karke apne console-log block ke NEECHE snap karo. Dono blocks ab ek stack bana lete hain — JavaScript pehle upar wala execute karega, fir neeche wala. Alert block ke text slot par click karke type karo: Hi there!
gui.tutorials.jsHello.dragHat: Flyout ke top par script block dhoondho — wahi jo hat shape mein hai. Use empty workspace ke middle mein DRAG karo. Yeh tumhara HAT block hai — yeh batata hai ki tumhara JavaScript program kahaan se shuru hota hai. Jaise hi tum drop karoge, right side mein Code tab dekho — wahaan tumhare JavaScript program ki shuruaat dikhegi. Jaise jaise tum aur blocks neeche jodte jaoge, Code tab mein real-time mein aur code aata jayega.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.dragLog: Ab flyout mein log-to-console block dhoondho aur use script hat ke neeche DRAG karo. Tumhe ek halki si shadow dikhegi jo bata rahi hogi ki kahaan snap hoga — wahin drop karna. Dono blocks ab ek dusre se chipak jaate hain, bilkul Scratch script ki tarah. Akela hat kuch nahi karta; neeche wala log block hi actually print karta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.jsCategory: Left side mein blocks panel dekho — JavaScript category already select hai tumhare liye, aur flyout har JavaScript block dikha raha hai jo tum use kar sakte ho. Bilkul upar, Entry label ke neeche, script block hai — wahi jo hat ki shape mein hai. Har JavaScript program isi se shuru hota hai. Agar tum kabhi dusri category par click kar do, toh wapas aane ke liye phir se JavaScript par click karna.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.openConsole: Run ke bilkul baju mein chhota >_ button dekha? Woh Console pane ko toggle karta hai. Us par click karo. View area ke bottom-right corner mein ek panel aata hai — wohi console hai. Real developers websites banate samay ise pure din open rakhte hain.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.progFilter: Extension Library bahut saare cards dikhata hai. JavaScript jaldi dhoondhne ke liye, upar Programming tab par click karo. Ab sirf programming-language extensions dikhenge — HTML 5, CSS, Python, C, aur JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.selectJs: JavaScript card par click karo. Yeh tumhare project mein poora JavaScript blocks ka set add kar dega — log-to-console, show-alert, variables, loops, sab kuch. Card band ho jata hai aur tum workspace par wapas aa jaate ho, ab blocks panel mein JavaScript category bhi hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.threeFriends: Webpages teen friends se milkar bante hain. HTML bones hai — shape. CSS paint hai — colours. JavaScript muscle hai — actions. Tumne Scratch mein sprites move kara hi hai. Ab tum ek webpage se kaam karwaoge, JavaScript blocks se jo Scratch blocks ki tarah hi dikhte hain.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.twoVoices: Tumne abhi do voices use ki. log-to-console silent hai — sirf developer dekhta hai, debugging ke liye perfect. show-alert loud hai — har visitor dekhta hai aur page freeze ho jata hai, toh ise sparingly use karna. Ab se har JavaScript chapter JavaScript extension ADD karke, Upload mode mein switch karke, aur inhi same blocks ko use karke shuru hota hai. Tumhare paas next program banane ke liye sab kuch hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.twoWays: JavaScript ke paas message dikhane ke do tareeke hain. CONSOLE ek hidden panel hai jo sirf developer dekhta hai — wahin debug karte hain. ALERT ek popup hai jo har visitor dekhta hai aur page ko freeze kar deta hai jab tak OK click na ho. Aaj hum DONO use karenge. Chalo project mein JavaScript blocks add karke shuru karein.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.typeMessage: Block ke text slot mein click karo jahaan message likha hai aur type karo: Hello JavaScript. Tum wahaan kuch bhi daal sakte ho — apna naam, koi joke, kuch bhi. Sirf text type karo — koi quotes nahi. Ab right side mein Code tab dekho: line padti hai console.log open-quote Hello JavaScript close-quote. Block ne tumhare text ko quotes mein automatically wrap kar diya. Agar tum real JavaScript haath se likh rahe hote, toh quotes khud lagani padti — yahi tareeka hai JavaScript ko batane ka ki value text hai, number nahi — par yeh kaam block tumhare liye kar deta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHello.uploadMode: Screen ke top-right corner mein toggle dekho. JavaScript extension add karte hi yeh already on ho gaya hai, toh tum abhi Upload mode mein hone chahiye — Code panel right side mein hai. Agar kisi vajah se tum Upload mode mein nahi ho, toh is toggle par click karke switch karo. Code panel aur View tab sirf Upload mode mein dikhte hain.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.buildPage: html, head, aur body drag karo. body ke andar, ek h1 daalo — id title, text My Page. Ek p daalo — id message, text Loading. body ke ant mein, URL script dot js ke saath ek script src block daalo.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.clickPage: index dot html row pe click karo. Dialog band ho jata hai. Workspace ab index dot html page sprite pe hai. Flyout HTML blocks dikhata hai. Code panel HTML pe switch karta hai. Same project, alag file.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.clickScript: Sprite switcher dialog phir kholo. script dot js row pe click karo. Workspace ab script dot js script sprite pe hai — khali workspace, flyout mein JavaScript blocks, code panel wait kar raha hai. Same project, alag file.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.grabAndChange: Script hat drag karo. Ab ek const block drag karo. Usse title naam do. Iski value ke liye, ek find element by id block daalo aur uska id title pe set karo — yeh aapke HTML page se h1 grab karta hai. Aage, ek set text of block drag karo. Element title hai, text Hello from JavaScript hai. Yeh page pe likha hua h1 badal dega.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.lookAtSprites: Dialog mein do rows hain. Pehli row mein index dot html HTML icon ke saath. Doosri row mein script dot js JavaScript icon ke saath. Kisi row pe click karo aur us sprite pe switch karo. Workspace, flyout, aur code panel sab follow karte hain.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.openIndex: View Files click karo. index dot html click karo. Browser page ko load karta hai, script src tag dekhta hai, script dot js fetch karta hai, use chalata hai. h1 tumhari aankhon ke saamne My Page se Hello from JavaScript mein badal jaata hai.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.patternForever: Ek project. Har file ke liye ek sprite. Files ke beech move karne ke liye sprite switcher dialog kholo. Har sprite ko save karo. index dot html page sprite pe switch karke View pe click karo unhe saath chalte dekhne ke liye. Yahaan se aage har DOM tutorial yahi pattern use karta hai — aap sirf script dot js sprite edit karte ho, phir page sprite pe wapas aakar result dekhte ho.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.savePage: Save pe click karo. File ka naam pehle se index hai aur extension pehle se dot html hai — sprite HTML hai, toh Save ko pehle se pata hai. Save pe click karo.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.saveScript: Save pe click karo. Default naam script hai aur extension pehle se dot js hai — script dot js sprite JavaScript hai, toh Save ko pehle se pata hai. Save pe click karo. Ab do files hain, ek doosre ke pass.
gui.tutorials.jsHtmlAndJs.twoFiles: Har asli website kam se kam do files mein rehti hai. Ek HTML file — wo PAGE hai jo aap dekhte ho. Aur ek JavaScript file — wo SCRIPT hai jo page ko cheezein karwati hai. HTML file ke andar ek special line hoti hai jo kehti hai: jab browser mujhe load kare, JavaScript file bhi load kare. Yahi tareeka hai dono files ka saath kaam karne ka: HTML page dikhata hai, page script ko load karta hai, script page ko hilata hai.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.applyPosi: After the four ifs, still inside the listener, two set style blocks. Property left on sprite, value is join x with px. Property top on sprite, value is join y with px. Setting CSS left and top is how you move an element on a webpage — different from Scratch where you set x and y directly.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.beyondMou: Scratch has when key pressed hat blocks — one per key. JavaScript has ONE event, keydown, that fires for any key. Inside, you check WHICH key. Every browser game uses this pattern. Today you move a sprite with arrow keys.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.clickScri: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Drag the script hat.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.fourIfs: Inside the keydown body, four ifs. If event key equals ArrowRight, change x by ten. ArrowLeft, change x by minus ten. ArrowDown, change y by ten. ArrowUp, change y by minus ten.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.keydownLi: Drag a when keydown on block. Element is document — the whole page. Event is keydown. The body runs every time ANY key is pressed.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.pageReady: The page sprite already has a div with id sprite — a tomato 40 by 40 box sitting at left 100, top 100. You edit the script sprite to make it move.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.saveAndPl: Click Save. Default name is script. Click Save. Then View Files, open index.html. Click into the preview so the page has focus. Press an arrow key. The sprite moves.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.setupStat: A const named sprite with find element by id and the text sprite. A let named x with value 100. A let named y with value 100. Two numbers track position — just like Scratch sprite x and y.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.threeLine: Listen for keys. Read which key. Update the position. Every keyboard-controlled browser game uses this shape.
gui.tutorials.jsKeyboardEvents.whichKey: Inside the listener, the event key reporter gives the name of the pressed key. The value is a string — ArrowRight, ArrowLeft, ArrowUp, ArrowDown, a, b, space, Enter. That is how you tell which key fired.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.askTheAge: Drag a let block. Name it age. Wrap a Number block around an ask user block asking How old are you. The Number block converts the text answer into a number for comparing.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.elseHalf: if block ko if-else block se replace karo. else body mein, You are too young log karo. Bilkul Scratch ke if-else jaisa — do branches, condition ke hisab se sirf ek chalega.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.ifShape: Control se ek if block drag karo. Slot condition hai — age greater-than-or-equal-to ten. Body wo hai jo condition true hone par chalega — ek log to console block jo bole You can join. Wahi C-shape jo tumne Scratch mein use ki thi.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.indentation: Look at the code panel. Lines inside the if body are indented by four spaces. Curly braces — open brace, close brace — mark where the body starts and ends. The indent is just to make it readable. Every JavaScript codebase indents this way.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.openConsole: Run ke baju mein >_ button click karo. Console pane right-bottom mein khulta hai. You can join dikhta hai — if branch chala.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.programsDeci: You know if and if-else from Scratch. JavaScript has the same two — just spelled if and else. Today you write the JS shape.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.runWithSeven: Click Run. Type seven. The check is false. The console shows You are too young. The else caught it.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.runWithTwelv: Click Run. Type twelve. The check is true.
gui.tutorials.jsMakeAChoice.twoPathsOneR: Same shape you know from Scratch — exactly one branch runs. The JS syntax is parentheses round the condition, curly braces round the body.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.clickScript: Click the script sprite. The workspace switches to JavaScript blocks. The code panel switches to JS mode. Same project, different file.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.foundationOfE: Every interactive page starts by grabbing an element. Once you have a reference, you can change its text, its style, listen for clicks. That comes next.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.grabMessage: Add another const block. Name it msg. Value is find element by id with the text message. Log msg. Save and reopen index.html. Two element references in hand.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.grabTitle: Drag the script hat. Drag a const block. Name it title. Value is find element by id with the text title. You just asked the page for the h1 with that id. This is the JS line — document dot getElementById.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.idMustMatch: One trap. The id in the block must match the id in your HTML exactly. Title with capital T will not find id title with small t. The page returns nothing.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.logTitle: Drag a log to console block. Drop title inside. The console will print the real h1 element.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. The pane shows the h1 element printed, not plain text. You grabbed a live piece of the page.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.pageIsTree: In Scratch you control sprites. On a webpage, you control HTML elements. The browser turns the HTML into a tree of objects called the DOM. JavaScript can grab any element from that tree and change it. That is how webpages come alive.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.saveAndOpen: Click Save. Default name is script, extension is already .js. Click Save. Then click View Files and open index.html. The browser fetches script.js and runs it.
gui.tutorials.jsMeetTheDom.twoSprites: Two sprites in the sprite selector. page already has the HTML — an h1 with id title and a p with id message. script is the empty JavaScript sprite. You only edit script today.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.askAge: Ek let block drag karo. Iska naam age rakho. Value slot mein, Dialogs section se ek ask user block daalo jo puchhe How old are you. Bilkul Scratch ke ask and wait jaisa — lekin dekho jab hum return aane wali cheez se maths karne ki koshish karte hain to kya hota hai.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.convertWithNumbe: Wrap the ask user block in a Number block. That converts the text to a real number. Click Run. Type ten.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.fiveTools: Tum Scratch ke maths operators jaante ho. JavaScript inhe plus add karne ke liye, minus subtract ke liye, star multiply ke liye, slash divide ke liye, aur percent divide karne ke baad bachne wale remainder ke liye likhta hai. Sirf plus mein surprise hai — jab dono side text ho, plus add karne ke bajaye strings ko glue kar deta hai. Yeh tutorial us surprise se guzarta hai aur use kaise fix karte hain woh batata hai.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.fiveToolsOneRule: The one rule to remember — wrap every ask user in a Number block before doing maths. Without it, plus glues text. With it, plus adds.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.moreMaths: Ek aur try karo — ek dusra log to console add karo: text In ten years you will be, plus age, plus ten. Run pe click karo aur ten type karo. Console ab do lines print karta hai. Pehli eleven hai; doosri twenty. Dono kaam ki kyunki age asli number hai, text nahi.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.openConsole: Run ke baju mein >_ button click karo. Console pane right-bottom mein khulta hai.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.openConsoleAgain: Open the console. Now the answer is eleven. The Number block fixed it.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.tryToAddOne: Drag a log to console block. Inside, join the words Next year you will be with age plus one. Click Run. Type ten.
gui.tutorials.jsNumbers.whyOneOhOne: Big JavaScript surprise. The ask user block hands back TEXT, not a number — even when you type ten. In JavaScript, plus between text glues. So ten and one became the text one-zero-one. In Scratch the answer block just works as a number; JavaScript does not.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.bracketForm: There is another way to reach a property — square brackets with the name in quotes. player open-bracket name close-bracket. Brackets help when the property name is in a variable.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.bundleRelated: Objects bundle related values together. Every game character, every quiz question, every JSON response from a server — all objects in real JavaScript.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.dotVsBracket: Arrays reach in by number. Objects reach in by name. Pick array for ordered same-shape data. Pick object for one labelled thing with named parts.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.labelsNotPositio: Scratch lists are positional — item one, item two, item three. JavaScript has another shape called an OBJECT. Inside, each value has a LABEL — player dot name, player dot score, player dot level. Scratch has nothing like this.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.makePlayer: Make an object called player with three pairs — name is Riya, score is eighty-five, level is three. Each pair is a label and a value. In JS this writes as curly braces with key colon value pairs inside.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.openConsole: Click the Console button next to Run. You see Riya and eighty-five. The property block reads a value by its label.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.readWithDot: Log property name of player. Then property score of player. In real JS, this is player dot name and player dot score. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.updateProperty: Set property score of player to ninety. Log player. Click Run. The whole object prints with score now ninety. The other properties are unchanged.
gui.tutorials.jsObjects.whenToUseWhich: Dot when you know the name in advance. Brackets when the name comes from a variable. Most of the time, dot.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.clickScript: Click the script sprite. JavaScript blocks in the flyout. Drag the script hat.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.firstRealForm: An input, a button, a handler that reads dot value and writes to the page. The shape of every signup form, every search box, every comment field on the web. dot value works on text fields, number fields, dropdowns, and checkboxes.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.goodbyePrompt: ask user is a popup — it interrupts the page. Real websites have input fields sitting on the page. JavaScript reads what the user typed using dot value. Today you build your first real form.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.grabThree: Three const blocks named nameInput, greetBtn, and out. Each value is find element by id with the matching id. Three references to page elements.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.pageReady: The page sprite already has an input with id nameInput, a button with id greetBtn and text Greet, and an empty p with id out. You edit the script sprite to wire the form.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.saveAndOpen: Click Save. Default name is script. Click Save. Then View Files, open index.html. Type a name in the box, click Greet. The paragraph fills with your greeting. No popup — the page itself responds.
gui.tutorials.jsReadInput.wireClick: Drag a when click on block. Element is greetBtn. Inside, a set text of block. Element out. Text joins Hello comma space, the get value of nameInput, and an exclamation. The get value block is JavaScript's dot value — it reads whatever the user typed.
gui.tutorials.jsStringsControl.lengthInc: Log text dot length — same as Scratch's length-of block, counts every character, spaces too. Then text dot includes with Java in quotes — returns true or false. Click Run.
gui.tutorials.jsStringsControl.makeText: Make a variable called text. Set it to two spaces, then Hello JavaScript, then two more spaces. Those spaces are part of the string. We will clean them up.
gui.tutorials.jsStringsControl.methodsLi: Scratch's strings extension has blocks like trim and replace as separate blocks. JavaScript builds them right onto every string. You call them with a dot — text dot trim. They are called methods.
gui.tutorials.jsStringsControl.neverChan: Big rule — every string method returns a NEW string. The original variable does not change. That is why you print or save the result — the method by itself leaves text untouched.
